title: feat: support AI-generated masks in shape_to_mask
body: Updated the export functionality to support AI-generated masks. The function now handles mask data from JSON files, including AI-generated mask formats (list/array and base64 encoded images).

step_plan.summary: Add support for AI-generated per-shape pixel masks in the export pipeline by extending `shape_to_mask`/`shapes_to_label` to accept and normalize mask data from JSON (nested lists/arrays and base64-encoded images), ensuring `export_json` produces correct label images while keeping existing vector-shape behavior unchanged.

steps.operation: Extend `shape_to_mask` to accept an optional precomputed mask and normalize supported mask encodings (nested lists/array-like, base64-encoded image, numpy arrays) into a boolean (H,W) ndarray; validate against `img_shape[:2]` and fall back to existing vector rasterization when no mask is provided.
steps.reason: `export_json` loads raw JSON directly (bypassing `LabelFile.load()` decoding), so `shape_to_mask` must be robust to raw `shape["mask"]` encodings and still support existing vector `points` + `shape_type` behavior without regressions.
steps.side_effects: May raise clearer errors for malformed/mismatched mask sizes; any downstream callers relying on implicit behavior (e.g., non-bool masks or wrong dimensions) may now fail fast. Ensure dtype/shape handling doesn’t break vector paths and that performance is acceptable for large masks.

steps.operation: Update `shapes_to_label` to detect `shape.get("mask")` and route it into `shape_to_mask` (with an explicit precedence rule: if `mask` is present and non-empty, use it; otherwise use vector rasterization); keep label mapping/instance id logic unchanged.
steps.reason: `shapes_to_label` is the orchestrator used by export; it currently assumes every shape is vector-based and always passes `points`/`shape_type`. It needs to pass through per-shape mask data so exports produce correct `label.png` for AI-generated masks.
steps.side_effects: If a shape includes both `points` and `mask`, the chosen precedence may change output versus older behavior; document/encode the rule. Potentially impacts any other consumers of `shapes_to_label` (not just export) by enabling mask-first handling.

steps.operation: Adjust export pipeline to ensure shapes loaded from raw JSON can include `mask` in either base64 or list/array form and still produce correct label outputs (e.g., by keeping JSON loading but ensuring the shapes dict is passed unchanged into `utils.shapes_to_label`, and adding any minimal compatibility glue if the CLI currently strips/normalizes fields).
steps.reason: The CLI path explicitly bypasses `LabelFile.load()` and thus sees un-decoded masks; after updating utils, `main()` must not inadvertently drop `mask` data and must keep the call chain (`main` → `shapes_to_label` → `shape_to_mask`) intact for both vector and AI-mask shapes.
steps.side_effects: Export outputs (`label.png`/derived artifacts) may change for datasets that already carried `mask` fields but were previously ignored; ensure backward compatibility for JSON without masks. May require updating CLI-level error handling/reporting when mask decoding/validation fails.
